Scope of Article 9.rule: Article 9 governs transactions that create a security interest in personal property or fixtures.
Scope of Article 9.application: Because the transaction creates a security interest in personal property, Article 9 applies.

Attachment — Requirements.rule: A security interest attaches when value is given, the debtor has rights in the collateral, and there is an authenticated security agreement or possession or control by the secured party.
Attachment — Requirements.application: Because value was given and the debtor authenticated a security agreement, attachment occurred.

Security Agreement.rule: A security agreement is an authenticated record that provides a description of the collateral.
Security Agreement.application: Because the agreement was authenticated and described the collateral, it is valid.

Description of Collateral.rule: Collateral is sufficiently described if it reasonably identifies what is described, but supergeneric descriptions are insufficient in a security agreement.
Description of Collateral.application: Because the collateral was specifically identified, the description is sufficient.

Value.rule: Value includes any consideration sufficient to support a simple contract or an antecedent debt.
Value.application: Because the loan satisfied an antecedent debt, value was given.

Debtor Rights in Collateral.rule: A debtor has rights in collateral when the debtor has an ownership interest or the power to transfer rights.
Debtor Rights in Collateral.application: Because the debtor owned the property, debtor rights existed.

Perfection — General Rule.rule: A security interest is perfected when it has attached and the secured party has taken all required steps.
Perfection — General Rule.application: Because attachment and filing occurred, the interest is perfected.

Perfection — Filing.rule: Perfection by filing requires a financing statement that provides the debtor’s name, the secured party’s name, and indicates the collateral.
Perfection — Filing.application: Because the financing statement listed the debtor and collateral, filing was effective.

Perfection — Debtor Name.rule: A financing statement must provide the debtor’s correct legal name.
Perfection — Debtor Name.application: Because the legal name was used, the filing is effective.

Perfection — Possession.rule: A secured party may perfect by possession of tangible collateral.
Perfection — Possession.application: Because the secured party took possession, perfection occurred.

Perfection — Control.rule: A security interest in deposit accounts, investment property, and electronic chattel paper may be perfected by control.
Perfection — Control.application: Because the bank had control of the account, perfection occurred.

Automatic Perfection — PMSI in Consumer.rule: A purchase-money security interest in consumer goods is automatically perfected upon attachment.
Automatic Perfection — PMSI in Consumer.application: Because the goods were consumer goods, perfection was automatic.

Collateral Classification.rule: Collateral is classified as consumer goods, inventory, equipment, or farm products based on the debtor’s use.
Collateral Classification.application: Because the goods were used in business, they are equipment.

Proceeds.rule: A security interest attaches to identifiable proceeds of collateral.
Proceeds.application: Because the proceeds were traceable, the interest attached.

Temporary Perfection — Proceeds.rule: A perfected security interest in proceeds remains perfected for 20 days.
Temporary Perfection — Proceeds.application: Because the proceeds were received within 20 days, perfection continued.

Priority — General Rule.rule: Priority among conflicting security interests is determined by the first to file or perfect.
Priority — General Rule.application: Because the creditor filed first, it has priority.

Priority — Unperfected Interests.rule: Between unperfected security interests, priority goes to the first to attach.
Priority — Unperfected Interests.application: Because attachment occurred earlier, priority applies.

Priority — Lien Creditors.rule: A perfected security interest has priority over a lien creditor.
Priority — Lien Creditors.application: Because the interest was perfected, it has priority over the lien.

Priority — PMSI in Goods.rule: A purchase-money security interest in goods has priority if perfected within the statutory time.
Priority — PMSI in Goods.application: Because the PMSI was timely perfected, it has priority.

Priority — Buyers in Ordinary Course.rule: A buyer in ordinary course of business takes free of a security interest created by the seller.
Priority — Buyers in Ordinary Course.application: Because the buyer purchased in ordinary course, the interest is cut off.

Default — Secured Party Rights.rule: Upon default, a secured party may repossess collateral without breach of the peace and dispose of it commercially reasonably.
Default — Secured Party Rights.application: Because repossession was peaceful, it was lawful.

Debtor Rights After Default.rule: A debtor has the right to redeem collateral by paying the full balance before disposition.
Debtor Rights After Default.application: Because the debtor tendered payment before sale, redemption occurred.

Deficiency and Surplus.rule: After disposition, the secured party may seek a deficiency or must return any surplus.
Deficiency and Surplus.application: Because sale proceeds were insufficient, a deficiency is owed.
